incentive.statement: General source topology must use one ordered three-value contract, preserve the separate Canvas Valence authority, and never turn unknown structural input into slot A or an authorized intransitive VNC.

coordinator.responsibility: Consolidate source-transitivity recognition and shell/URL parity without merging structural A/B/C topology with Canvas Valence or binary Lesson 4 formula grouping.

outcome: One generation-valency contract owns intransitive/transitive/bitransitive order, A/B/C topology, and vi/vt/vb aliases; invalid explicit or restored input remains blocked and cannot authorize or select a fallback source slot.

responsibilities.statement: Own the ordered vocabulary, aliases, typed recognition, structural slot mapping, and binary Canvas formula projection.

actors.contribution: Defined the neutral source-topology contract and removed invalid-to-intransitive Lesson 4 authorization.

responsibilities.statement: Consume typed source-transitivity frames in composer, URL restoration, and rendering without falling through invalid explicit intent.

actors.contribution: Derived UI constants and aliases from the core contract, retained invalid diagnostics, and blocked malformed URL application.

responsibilities.statement: Prove all three visible groups, hidden select, A/B/C slot identity, URL aliases, hostile input, runtime graph, and live refresh behavior.

actors.contribution: Added exact parity and hostile tests, cache-busted the changed graph, and verified browser restore behavior.

responsibilities.statement: Record the bounded comparison, intentional exclusions, validation evidence, and remaining main findings.

actors.contribution: Maintained the shared-action boundary and handoff reminder.

contracts.invariant: Absent is distinct from invalid; only recognized source transitivity selects A/B/C, aliases normalize canonically, and Canvas Valence remains the independent grammar-authorizing choice.

retirements.removalEvidence: Hostile URL, renderer, composer-semantic, and Lesson 4 tests now retain invalid intent as blocked, select no A/B/C slot, and produce no formula or serialized source.

closure.detail: Independent handwritten recognition and invalid-to-intransitive slot-A fallback no longer decide source topology; the typed generation-valency contract is canonical.

evidence: Focused proof passed: UI 411/411; state, orthography, and inline composer 462/462; VNC 213/213; application plus grammar-contract registry 75/75.
Exact parity proof compares all three visible transitivity groups, the hidden state select, and A/B/C slot shells against the core order; a poisoned group blocks the inventory frame.
Hostile proof rejects malformed /tr/fabricated restoration, invalid explicit renderer input, invalid composer semantics, and invalid Lesson 4 VNC selection without formula or slot fallback.

Live browser entry 051 restored bitransitive slot C while Canvas Valence remained independently intransitive; forged transitivity selected no tab, consumed no source slot, produced no formula, and emitted no warning or error logs.

Runtime manifest remained one browser entry with 75 modules and 75 installers; syntax, JSON parsing, and diff checks passed. Lessons 20 through 22 retained only its pre-existing unrelated wording failure at 29/30.

Alignment architecture metrics matched baseline exactly: responsibility actor 0/0, renderer construction 38/38, optional renderer dependency 0/0, registered-contract coverage 200/200, and browser scripts 1/1. Remaining failures are inherited board size and cross-action dirty-worktree overlap.
